arts.ttl: Barcelona President Joan Laporta charged with alleged fraud for second time in two weeks
arts.sum: Recently, Barcelona president Joan Laporta, as well as his former partner Joan Oliver, were charged by a Barcelona magistrate for allegedly failing to return an investment of €4.7m to another investor. The pair are now facing trouble again. As reported by Relevo, the Court of Instruction number 21 of Barcelona has admitted a complaint filed […] The post Barcelona President Joan Laporta charged with alleged fraud for second time in two weeks appeared first on Football España.

arts.ttl: Barcelona vs Sevilla: Preview, predictions and lineups
arts.sum: Sunday's visitors currently sit 12th in La Liga despite their upsurge before the international break, and they secured a huge victory over Real Betis in the Seville Derby last time out. La Liga leaders Barcelona are back in domestic action on Sunday night as they welcome a resurgent Sevilla to Catalonia.

arts.ttl: Man United dealt double transfer blow as Barcelona president confirms contract talks
arts.sum: Barcelona president Joan Laporta confirms that the La Liga leaders are discussing new contracts with Frenkie de Jong, Ronald Araujo and Pedri.

arts.ttl: Predicted XIs Barcelona-Sevilla: Lamine Yamal to start despite injury scare
arts.sum: Barcelona were pegged back by Real Madrid on Saturday, thanks to Carlo Ancelotti’s side winning 2-1 at Celta Vigo. The Catalans will aim to re-establish their three-point lead at the top of La Liga when they take on Sevilla at the Estadi Olimpic. arts.ttl: Barcelona president confirms contract talks with 3 key players
arts.sum: Speaking during his opening speech at FC Barcelona's Ordinary General Assembly, club president Laporta confirmed talks are ongoing between Barca and all three players' representatives, revealing: “We are working on Araujo, De Jong and Pedri’s new contracts.” Barcelona president Joan Laporta has confirmed that the club are in talks with Ronald Araujo, Pedri and Frenkie de Jong over new contracts.

arts.ttl: Barcelona possible starting lineup against Sevilla in LaLiga
arts.sum: Hansi Flick has confirmed that Gavi, Dani Olmo, and Fermin will be fit for the match . The German coach also confirmed that Iñaki Peña will remain the starting goalkeeper, though he expressed optimism about Szczesny’s return to fitness.
